FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">As of December 31, 2022, in addition to the <ix:nonFraction id="fid_680" name="cosm:AdditionalSharesIssuedShares" contextRef="AsOf2021-12-31_cosm_ShareExchangeAgreementMember_cosm_IccMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="Shares" decimals="0">3,000,000</ix:nonFraction> ICC shares valued at $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_682" name="cosm:AdditionalSharesIssuedAmount" contextRef="AsOf2021-12-31_cosm_ShareExchangeAgreementMember_cosm_IccMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">0</ix:nonFraction>, as noted above, marketable securities also consisted of the following: <ix:nonFraction id="fid_697" name="cosm:SharesIssuedAsMarketableSecurities" contextRef="AsOf2022-12-31_cosm_MarketableSecuritiesNationalBanksOfGreeceMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="Shares" decimals="0">16,666</ix:nonFraction> shares which traded at a closing price of $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_693" name="cosm:ClosingPriceOfShares" contextRef="AsOf2022-12-31_cosm_MarketableSecuritiesNationalBanksOfGreeceMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USDPShares" decimals="INF">0.40</ix:nonFraction> per share or value of $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_695" name="us-gaap:MarketableSecurities" contextRef="AsOf2022-12-31_cosm_MarketableSecuritiesNationalBanksOfGreeceMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">6,680</ix:nonFraction> of National Bank of Greece. Additionally, the Company has $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_699" name="us-gaap:StockIssuedDuringPeriodValueOther" contextRef="From2022-01-01to2022-12-31_cosm_MarketableSecuritiesDivsersaSaMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">8,200</ix:nonFraction> in equity securities of Pancreta Bank, which are revalued annually. The Company recorded a net unrealized gain on the fair value of these investments of $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_709" name="us-gaap:UnrealizedGainLossOnInvestments" contextRef="From2022-01-01to2022-12-31_cosm_ShareExchangeAgreementMember_cosm_IccMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">1,676</ix:nonFraction> during the year ended December 31, 2022.</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;"><em>CosmoFarmacy LP</em></p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">In September 2019, the Company entered into an agreement with an unaffiliated third party to incorporate CosmoFarmacy L.P. for the purpose of providing strategic management consulting services and the retail trade of pharmaceutical products, and OTC to pharmacies. CosmoFarmacy was incorporated with a 30-year term through May 31, 2049. The unaffiliated third party is the general partner (the &#8220;GP&#8221;) of the limited partnership and is responsible for management and decision-making associated with CosmoFarmacy. The initial share capital was set to EUR <ix:nonFraction id="fid_683" name="cosm:InitialShareCapital" contextRef="From2022-01-01to2022-12-31_cosm_CosmoFarmacyLPMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="EUR" decimals="0">150,000</ix:nonFraction> which was later increased to EUR <ix:nonFraction id="fid_684" name="cosm:InitialShareCapitalIncreased" contextRef="From2022-01-01to2022-12-31_cosm_CosmoFarmacyLPMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="EUR" decimals="0">500,000</ix:nonFraction>. The GP contributed the pharmacy license (the &#8220;License&#8221;) valued at EUR <ix:nonFraction id="fid_685" name="cosm:PharmacyLicenseValue" contextRef="From2022-01-01to2022-12-31_cosm_CosmoFarmacyLPMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="EUR" decimals="0">350,000</ix:nonFraction> (<ix:nonNumeric id="fid_686" name="cosm:MaturityPeriodOfLicense" contextRef="From2022-01-01to2022-12-31_cosm_CosmoFarmacyLPMember" format="ixt-sec:duryear">30</ix:nonNumeric>-year term) to operate the business of CosmoFarmacy in exchange for a <ix:nonFraction id="fid_687" name="cosm:EquityMethodInvestmentsOwnershipPercentage" contextRef="AsOf2019-09-30_cosm_CosmoFarmacyLPMember" unitRef="Pure" decimals="INF" scale="-2">70</ix:nonFraction>% equity ownership. The Company is a limited partner and contributed cash of EUR <ix:nonFraction id="fid_688" name="cosm:CashContributedToLimitedPartner" contextRef="From2022-01-01to2022-12-31_cosm_CosmoFarmacyLPMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="EUR" decimals="0">150,000</ix:nonFraction> for the remaining <ix:nonFraction id="fid_689" name="cosm:EquityMethodInvestmentOwnershipRemainingPercentage" contextRef="AsOf2019-09-30_cosm_CosmoFarmacyLPMember" unitRef="Pure" decimals="INF" scale="-2">30</ix:nonFraction>% equity ownership. CosmoFarmacy is not publicly traded and the Company&#8217;s investment has been recorded using the equity method of accounting. The value of the investment as of December 31, 2022 and 2021, was $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_710" name="us-gaap:Investments" contextRef="AsOf2022-12-31_cosm_CosmoFarmacyLPMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">160,470</ix:nonFraction> and $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_711" name="us-gaap:Investments" contextRef="AsOf2021-12-31_cosm_CosmoFarmacyLPMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">169,770</ix:nonFraction>, respectively, and is included in &#8220;Other assets&#8221; on the Company&#8217;s consolidated balance sheets.</p></ix:continuation><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><ix:nonNumeric id="fid_951" style="height:15px"><td></td></tr></tbody></table><p style="font-size:10pt;font-family:times new roman;margin:0px">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:center;"><strong>COSMOS HEALTH INC.</strong></p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:center;"><strong>Notes to the Consolidated Financial Statements</strong></p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:center;"><strong>December 31, 2022</strong></p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:center;">&#160;</p><ix:nonNumeric id="fid_953" name="cosm:LoanReceivabledisclosuretextblock" contextRef="From2022-01-01to2022-12-31" escape="true"><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;"><strong>NOTE 6 &#8211; LOAN RECEIVABLE</strong></p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">On October 30, 2021, the Company entered into an agreement for a ten-year loan with Medihelm SA to memorialize &#8364;4,284,521 ($<ix:nonFraction id="fid_745" name="us-gaap:PaymentsOfLoanCosts" contextRef="From2021-10-01to2021-10-30" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">4,849,221</ix:nonFraction>) in prepayments the Company had made. The prepayments to Medihelm SA had been made in accordance with the parallel export business, through which Medihelm supplied and would supply SkyPharm SA with branded pharmaceuticals. This business is no longer in place for the Company and thus the Company entered into this agreement with Medihelm SA in order for the outstanding amount to be settled. Interest is calculated at a rate of <ix:nonFraction id="fid_746" name="us-gaap:ShortTermDebtInterestRateIncrease" contextRef="From2021-10-01to2021-10-30" unitRef="Pure" decimals="INF" scale="-2">5.5</ix:nonFraction>% per annum on a <ix:nonNumeric id="fid_750" name="cosm:ThresholdPeriodPastDueForWriteoffsOfFinancingReceivable" contextRef="From2021-10-01to2021-10-30" format="ixt-sec:durday">360</ix:nonNumeric>-day basis. Under the terms of the agreement, <ix:nonNumeric id="fid_751" name="cosm:DecriptionOfLoanPayment" contextRef="From2021-10-01to2021-10-30">the Company is to receive 120 equal payments over the term of the loan</ix:nonNumeric>. As of December 31, 2021, the Company had a short-term receivable balance of $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_748" name="us-gaap:InterestReceivable" contextRef="AsOf2021-12-31" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">377,590</ix:nonFraction> and a long-term receivable balance of $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_749" name="us-gaap:NontradeReceivables" contextRef="AsOf2021-12-31" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">4,410,689</ix:nonFraction> under this loan. During the year ended December 31, 2022, the Company received &#8364;333,619 ($<ix:nonFraction id="fid_747" name="us-gaap:DebtInstrumentPeriodicPayment" contextRef="From2022-01-01to2022-12-31" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">356,906</ix:nonFraction>) in principal payments such that as of December 31, 2022, the Company had a short-term receivable balance of $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_752" name="us-gaap:InterestReceivable" contextRef="AsOf2022-12-31" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">377,038</ix:nonFraction> and a long-term receivable balance of $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_753" name="us-gaap:NontradeReceivables" contextRef="AsOf2022-12-31" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">3,792,034</ix:nonFraction> under this loan. The Company also received &#8364;190,734($<ix:nonFraction id="fid_754" name="us-gaap:InterestPaid" contextRef="From2022-01-01to2022-12-31" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">204,047</ix:nonFraction>) in interest payments during year ended December 31, 2022. </p></ix:nonNumeric><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><ix:nonNumeric id="fid_954" name="us-gaap:PartnersCapitalNotesDisclosureTextBlock" contextRef="From2022-01-01to2022-12-31" escape="true" continuedAt="cont_d22d05"><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;"><strong>NOTE&#160;7 &#8211; CAPITAL STRUCTURE</strong></p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;"><em>Preferred Stock</em></p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">The Company is authorized to issue <ix:nonFraction id="fid_1898" name="us-gaap:PreferredStockSharesAuthorized" contextRef="AsOf2022-12-31" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="Shares" decimals="0">100</ix:nonFraction> million shares of preferred stock, of which <ix:nonFraction id="fid_1941" name="cosm:PreferredStockLiquidationPreference1" contextRef="AsOf2022-12-31_us-gaap_SeriesAPreferredStockMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="Shares" decimals="0">6,000,000</ix:nonFraction> are designated as Series A convertible preferred stock. The preferred stock has a&#160;liquidation preference over the common stock and&#160;is non-voting. As of December 31, 2022 and 2021, no preferred shares were issued and outstanding.</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;"><em>Major Rights &amp; Preferences of Series A Preferred Stock</em></p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">On and effective October 4, 2021, the Company amended and restated its articles of incorporation (the &#8220;Amended and Restated Articles&#8221;) and filed a certificate of designation (the &#8220;COD&#8221;) for its Series A Preferred Stock (the &#8220;Series A Preferred Stock&#8221;) with the State of Nevada. The Amended and Restated Articles allow the Company&#8217;s Board of Directors the authority to authorize the issuance of preferred stock from time to time in one or more classes or series by resolution. On February 23, 2022, the Company filed Correction No. 1 to the COD. On July 28, 2022, the Company filed an Amendment to the COD with the State of Nevada to allow a holder to waive application of the Beneficial Ownership Limitation with respect to the conversion of Series A Preferred Stock.</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">With respect to payment of dividends and distribution of assets upon liquidation, dissolution, or winding up of the Company, whether voluntary or involuntary, all shares of the Series A Preferred Stock will rank: (i) senior to all of the Company&#8217;s Common Stock and any other equity securities that the Company may issue in the future, (ii) equal to any other equity securities that the Company may issue in the future, the terms of which specifically provide that such equity securities are on parity or senior to the Series A Preferred Stock (&#8220;Parity Securities&#8221;), (iii) junior to all other equity securities the Company issues, the terms of which specifically provide that such equity securities rank senior to the Series A Preferred Stock, and (iv) junior to all of the Company&#8217;s existing and future indebtedness; without the prior written consent of the Majority Holders.&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">In the event of any voluntary or involuntary liquidation, dissolution or winding up of the Company (a &#8220;Liquidation&#8221;), the Holders of shares of Series A Preferred Stock shall be first entitled to receive out of the assets of the Company available for distribution to its shareholders.</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">Each Holder shall not be entitled to vote with holders of outstanding shares of Common Stock, voting together as a single class, with respect to any and all matters presented to the stockholders of the Company for their action or consideration, except as provided by law or as set forth in the COD.&#160; The holders of Series A Preferred Stock are entitled to receive dividends paid and distributions made to the holders of Common Stock to the same extent as if the holders of Series A Preferred Stock had converted such shares into shares of Common Stock.</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">The <ix:nonNumeric id="fid_1958" name="cosm:DescriptionOfConvertibleIntoCommonStock" contextRef="From2022-01-01to2022-12-31_us-gaap_SeriesAPreferredStockMember">Series A Preferred Stock was initially convertible into the Company&#8217;s Common Stock as determined by dividing the number of shares of Series A Preferred Stock to be converted by the lower of (i) $75.00 or (ii) 80% of the average volume weighted average price for the Company&#8217;s Common Stock for the five (5) trading days immediately following the effectiveness of the registration statement concerning the shares (the &#8220;Conversion Price&#8221;). On June 14, 2022, the Conversion Price was reset to $15.54 per share.</ix:nonNumeric></p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">Each holder is entitled to receive dividends in shares of Series A Preferred Stock or cash determined based on the stated value of each Series A Preferred Stock at the dividend rate of <ix:nonFraction id="fid_1959" name="us-gaap:PreferredStockDividendRatePercentage" contextRef="From2022-01-01to2022-12-31_us-gaap_SeriesAPreferredStockMember" unitRef="Pure" decimals="INF" scale="-2">8.0</ix:nonFraction>% per year. For the year ended December 31, 2022, the Company recorded $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1960" name="us-gaap:InvestmentIncomeDividend" contextRef="From2022-01-01to2022-12-31_us-gaap_SeriesAPreferredStockMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">372,414</ix:nonFraction> as a deemed dividend in accordance with the Series A Preferred Stock cumulative dividend. As of December 31, 2022, the cumulative dividend has been recorded as mezzanine equity. The Company expects to pay the aforementioned dividend in shares of the Company&#8217;s common stock during the year ended December 31, 2023, upon which the total dividend value will be reclassified from mezzanine to permanent equity.</p></ix:nonNumeric><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><table style="border-spacing:0;font:10pt times new roman;width:100%" cellpadding="0"><tbody><tr style="height:15px"><td class="hpbhr"></td></tr><tr style="height:15px"><td style="BORDER-BOTTOM: black 1px solid; TEXT-INDENT: 0px;text-align:center;">F-18</td></tr><tr style="height:15px"><td><p style='page-break-after: always'></p></td></tr><tr style="height:15px"><td></td></tr></tbody></table><p style="font-size:10pt;font-family:times new roman;margin:0px">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:center;"><strong>COSMOS HEALTH INC.</strong></p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:center;"><strong>Notes to the Consolidated Financial Statements</strong></p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:center;"><strong>December 31, 2022</strong></p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:center;">&#160;</p><ix:continuation id="cont_d22d05" continuedAt="cont_7f1d78"><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">On February 28, 2022, the Company entered into a securities purchase agreement, or the Purchase Agreement, with certain investors and an insider for a private placement of the Company&#8217;s securities (the &#8220;Private Placement&#8221;).</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="font-size:10pt;font-family:times new roman;margin:0px">The Private Placement consisted of the sale of&#160;<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1944" name="cosm:SalesOfShares" contextRef="From2022-02-01to2022-02-28_us-gaap_SeriesAPreferredStockMember_us-gaap_PrivatePlacementMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="Shares" decimals="0">6,000</ix:nonFraction>&#160;shares of the Company&#8217;s Series A Convertible Preferred Stock, or the Series A Shares, at a price of $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1961" name="cosm:SharesPricePerShare" contextRef="From2022-02-01to2022-02-28_us-gaap_SeriesAPreferredStockMember_us-gaap_PrivatePlacementMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USDPShares" decimals="INF">1,000</ix:nonFraction> per share, and&#160;<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1945" name="cosm:WarrantsToPurchaseSharesOfCommonStock" contextRef="From2022-02-01to2022-02-28_us-gaap_SeriesAPreferredStockMember_us-gaap_PrivatePlacementMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="Shares" decimals="0">80,000</ix:nonFraction>&#160;warrants to purchase shares of common stock, or the Warrants, for aggregate gross proceeds of approximately $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1946" name="cosm:AggregateGrossProceedsWarrants" contextRef="From2022-02-01to2022-02-28_us-gaap_SeriesAPreferredStockMember_us-gaap_PrivatePlacementMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="-6" scale="6">6</ix:nonFraction>&#160;million. <ix:nonNumeric id="fid_1947" name="cosm:DescriptionOfWarrantsExercisable" contextRef="From2022-02-01to2022-02-28_us-gaap_SeriesAPreferredStockMember_us-gaap_PrivatePlacementMember">The Warrants were initially exercisable to purchase shares of common stock at $82.50 per share, or 110% of the Series A Shares initial conversion price and will expire five and one-half years following the initial exercise date of the Warrants</ix:nonNumeric>. The Company determined that the 80,000 warrants are additional value being distributed to the preferred stockholders and presented the warrants&#8217; fair value of $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1951" name="us-gaap:Dividends" contextRef="From2022-02-01to2022-02-28_us-gaap_SeriesAPreferredStockMember_us-gaap_PrivatePlacementMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">5,788,493</ix:nonFraction> as a deemed dividend on issuance of warrants in the consolidated statements of operations and comprehensive loss. <ix:nonNumeric id="fid_1948" name="cosm:DescriptionOfWarrantsBlackScholesOptionPricingModelExercisable" contextRef="From2022-02-01to2022-02-28_us-gaap_SeriesAPreferredStockMember_us-gaap_PrivatePlacementMember">The warrants were valued using the Black-Scholes option pricing model with the following terms: a) exercise price of $82.50, b) common stock fair value of $85.50, c) volatility of 118%, d) discount rate of 1.71%, e) term of 5.50 years and f) dividend rate of 0%.</ix:nonNumeric></p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">The closing of the Private Placement occurred on February 28, 2022. As a condition to the closing of the sale, the Company&#8217;s common stock received conditional approval for listing and trading on the Nasdaq Capital Market and commenced trading on February 28, 2022, under the trading symbol, COSM. Concurrent with the issuance of the Series A Shares, the Company executed a registration rights agreement (the &#8220;Registration Rights Agreement&#8221;) to register the resale of the shares of common stock issuable upon conversion of the Series A Shares and the shares of common stock issuable upon exercise of the warrants issued in connection with the Series A Shares. The Company was required to file its initial registration statement within 45 days following February 28, 2022. The Effectiveness Date was required to be 60 days after February 28, 2022, or 75 days following the SEC&#8217;s full review, and any additional registration statements that may be required are to be filed within 20 days following the date required by the SEC. If the Company fails to timely file its initial registration statement, or any additional registration statement, or otherwise comply with the requirements of the Registration Rights Agreement, the Company shall pay each holder 2% of the subscription amount in cash until cured, with an additional penalty of 18% if the cash payment is not made within seven days of the cash payable date.</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">The Company filed its initial registration statement on May 25, 2022, and thus accrued for liquidated damages payable to the Holders in the amount of $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1903" name="cosm:LiquidatedDamagesPayable" contextRef="AsOf2022-05-25" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">250,260</ix:nonFraction>, calculated as described above, for both the late filing of the registration statement (event) and <ix:nonNumeric id="fid_1962" name="cosm:DescriptionOfLateFilingOfRegistrationStatement" contextRef="From2022-05-01to2022-05-25">the 1st anniversary (30 days following the event date) of the event which along with an additional lumpsum amount of $2,000,000 agreed to paid to the investors as additional damages led to a total amount of $2,250,260 concerning liquidated damages related to the February Private Placement within the year ended December 31, 2022</ix:nonNumeric>. Upon the effectiveness of the Company&#8217;s registration statement, the Series A Shares conversion price was adjusted to $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1912" name="us-gaap:DebtInstrumentConvertibleConversionPrice1" contextRef="AsOf2022-05-25" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USDPShares" decimals="INF">15.54</ix:nonFraction>&#160;and the warrant exercise price was adjusted to $15.54 per share. The Company recorded a deemed dividend in the amount of $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1904" name="us-gaap:InvestmentIncomeDividend" contextRef="From2022-05-01to2022-05-25" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">8,189,515</ix:nonFraction> upon reducing the conversion price <ix:nonNumeric id="fid_1906" name="us-gaap:ConversionOfStockDescription" contextRef="From2022-05-01to2022-05-25">from $75.00 to $15.54</ix:nonNumeric> which was recorded as an increase to additional paid-in capital and an increase to accumulated deficit.</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">The Series A Shares rank senior to all of the Company&#8217;s Common Stock and any other equity securities that the Company may issue in the future with respect to payment of dividends and distribution of assets upon liquidation, dissolution or winding up. While the Series A Shares are outstanding, the Company may not amend, alter or change adversely the powers, preferences or rights given to the Series A Shares, create, or authorize the creation of, any additional class or series of capital stock of the Company (or any security convertible into or exercisable for any class or series of capital stock of the Company), including any class or series of capital stock of the Company that ranks superior to or in parity with the Series A Shares, alter, amend, modify, or repeal its Articles of Incorporation or other charter documents in any manner that adversely affects any rights of the holders of Series A Shares, increase or decrease the number of authorized shares of Series A Shares, any agreement, commitment or transaction that would result in a Change of Control, any sale or disposition of any material assets outside of the ordinary course of business of the Company, any material change in the principal business of the Company, including the entry into any new line of business or exit of any current line of business, and circumvent a right or preference of the Series A Shares. Any holder of the Series A Shares shall have the right by written election to the Company to convert all or any portion of the outstanding Series A Shares. Immediately upon effectiveness of a registration statement registering for resale all of the Registrable Securities (as defined in the Registration Rights Agreement), all outstanding Series A Shares shall automatically convert into Common Stock, subject to certain beneficial ownership limitations. </p></ix:continuation><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><table style="border-spacing:0;font:10pt times new roman;width:100%" cellpadding="0"><tbody><tr style="height:15px"><td class="hpbhr"></td></tr><tr style="height:15px"><td style="BORDER-BOTTOM: black 1px solid; TEXT-INDENT: 0px;text-align:center;">F-19</td></tr><tr style="height:15px"><td><p style='page-break-after: always'></p></td></tr><tr style="height:15px"><td></td></tr></tbody></table><p style="font-size:10pt;font-family:times new roman;margin:0px">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:center;"><strong>COSMOS HEALTH INC.</strong></p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:center;"><strong>Notes to the Consolidated Financial Statements</strong></p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:center;"><strong>December 31, 2022</strong></p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:center;">&#160;</p><ix:continuation id="cont_7f1d78" continuedAt="cont_b2d403"><p style="font-size:10pt;font-family:times new roman;margin:0px"><em>Mezzanine Equity</em></p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">The Series A Shares are recorded as mezzanine equity in accordance with ASC 480 at its initial net carrying value in the amount of $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1924" name="cosm:SeriesAPreferredStock" contextRef="AsOf2022-12-31" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">5,452,300</ix:nonFraction>. The Series A Shares are recorded as mezzanine equity in accordance with ASC 480 as the Company may be obligated to issue a variable number of shares at a fixed price known at inception and there is no maximum number of shares that could potentially be issued upon conversion. In this instance, cash settlement would be presumed and the Series A Shares are classified as mezzanine equity in accordance with ASC 480-10-S99. Immediately upon effectiveness of the registration statement registering for resale of all the common stock issuable under the Series A Shares, all outstanding Series A Shares shall automatically convert into common stock.</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">As of December 31, 2022, <ix:nonFraction id="fid_1914" name="cosm:ConvertibleStock" contextRef="From2022-01-01to2022-12-31" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="Shares" decimals="0">6,000</ix:nonFraction> of the Series A Shares had been converted into <ix:nonFraction id="fid_1915" name="us-gaap:ConversionOfStockSharesIssued1" contextRef="From2022-01-01to2022-12-31" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="Shares" decimals="0">386,588</ix:nonFraction> shares of common stock in accordance with the terms of the agreements and thus an amount of $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1916" name="us-gaap:ConversionOfStockAmountConverted1" contextRef="From2022-01-01to2022-12-31" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">5,452,300</ix:nonFraction> was reclassified from mezzanine equity to common stock and additional paid-in capital, in the aggregate. </p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;"><em>Common Stock</em></p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">The Company is authorized to issue 300 million shares of common stock. As of December 31, 2022 and 2021, the Company had <ix:nonFraction id="fid_1920" name="us-gaap:CommonStockSharesIssued" contextRef="AsOf2022-12-31" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="Shares" decimals="0">10,605,412</ix:nonFraction> and <ix:nonFraction id="fid_1921" name="us-gaap:CommonStockSharesIssued" contextRef="AsOf2021-12-31" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="Shares" decimals="0">701,780</ix:nonFraction> shares of our common stock issued, respectively, and <ix:nonFraction id="fid_1922" name="us-gaap:CommonStockSharesOutstanding" contextRef="AsOf2022-12-31" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="Shares" decimals="0">10,589,915</ix:nonFraction> and <ix:nonFraction id="fid_1923" name="us-gaap:CommonStockSharesOutstanding" contextRef="AsOf2021-12-31" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="Shares" decimals="0">686,283</ix:nonFraction> shares outstanding, respectively.</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;"><span style="text-decoration:underline">Sale of Treasury Shares</span></p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">On February 5, 2021, the Company entered into a Stock Purchase Agreement (the &#8220;February SPA&#8221;) with an unaffiliated third-party. With the February SPA the Company proceeded to sell <ix:nonFraction id="fid_1963" name="us-gaap:TreasuryStockCommonShares" contextRef="AsOf2021-02-05_cosm_StockPurchaseAgreementMember_cosm_TreasuryStocksMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="Shares" decimals="0">2,600</ix:nonFraction> shares of the Company&#8217;s common stock held in treasury at $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1965" name="cosm:SaleOfTreasuryStocksParValue" contextRef="AsOf2021-02-05_cosm_StockPurchaseAgreementMember_cosm_TreasuryStocksMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USDPShares" decimals="INF">96.25</ix:nonFraction> per share or a total of $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1964" name="us-gaap:TreasuryStockCommonValue" contextRef="AsOf2021-02-05_cosm_StockPurchaseAgreementMember_cosm_TreasuryStocksMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">250,000</ix:nonFraction>.&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;"><span style="text-decoration:underline">Cancellation of Treasury Shares</span></p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; Income provision (GILTI) have the option to account for the GILTI tax as a period cost if and when incurred, or to recognize deferred taxes for outside basis temporary differences expected to reverse as GILTI. We have elected to account for GILTI as a period cost.</p></ix:nonNumeric><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><table style="border-spacing:0;font:10pt times new roman;width:100%" cellpadding="0"><tbody><tr style="height:15px"><td class="hpbhr"></td></tr><tr style="height:15px"><td style="BORDER-BOTTOM: black 1px solid; TEXT-INDENT: 0px;text-align:center;">F-24</td></tr><tr style="height:15px"><td><p style='page-break-after: always'></p></td></tr><tr style="height:15px"><td></td></tr></tbody></table><p style="font-size:10pt;font-family:times new roman;margin:0px">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:center;"><strong>COSMOS HEALTH INC.</strong></p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:center;"><strong>Notes to the Consolidated Financial Statements</strong></p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:center;"><strong>December 31, 2022</strong></p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:center;">&#160;</p><ix:continuation id="cont_7a9722"><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">Deferred income taxes reflect the net tax effects of temporary differences between the carrying amount of assets and liabilities for financial reporting purposes and amounts used for income tax purposes. Significant components of the Company&#8217;s deferred tax assets and liabilities consist of the following:</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><ix:nonNumeric id="fid_976" name="us-gaap:ScheduleOfDeferredTaxAssetsAndLiabilitiesTableTextBlock" contextRef="From2022-01-01to2022-12-31" escape="true"><table FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">At December 31, 2022, the Company had U.S. net operating loss (&#8220;NOL&#8221;) carryforwards of approximately $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1893" name="us-gaap:DeferredTaxAssetsOperatingLossCarryforwardsStateAndLocal" contextRef="AsOf2022-12-31" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">17,169,419</ix:nonFraction> that may be offset against future taxable income, <ix:nonNumeric id="fid_1891" name="cosm:FederalStatutoryIncomeTaxRateDescription" contextRef="From2022-01-01to2022-12-31">subject to limitation under IRC Section 382. Of the $17.2 million Federal NOL carryforwards, $2.5 million are pre-2018 and begin to expire in 2031. The remaining balance of $4.6 million, are limited to utilization of 80% of taxable income but do not have an expiration</ix:nonNumeric>. At December 31, 2022, the Company had Greek NOL carryforwards of $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1894" name="us-gaap:DeferredTaxAssetsOperatingLossCarryforwardsStateAndLocal" contextRef="AsOf2022-12-31_cosm_UnitedStatesMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">87,384</ix:nonFraction> and has UK NOL carryforwards of $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1896" name="us-gaap:DeferredTaxAssetsOperatingLossCarryforwardsStateAndLocal" contextRef="AsOf2022-12-31_cosm_UkMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">1,450,783</ix:nonFraction>. A valuation allowance exists for all operations, based on a more likely than not criterion and in consideration of all available positive and negative evidence.</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">ASC 740 requires that the tax benefit of net operating losses (&#8220;NOLs&#8221;), temporary differences and credit carryforwards be recorded as an asset to the extent that management assesses that realization is &#8220;more likely than not.&#8221; Realization of the future tax benefits is dependent on the Company&#8217;s ability to generate sufficient taxable income within the carryforward period. Because of the Company&#8217;s history of domestic operating losses, management believes that recognition of the deferred tax assets arising from the above-mentioned future tax benefits is currently not likely to be realized and, accordingly, has provided a valuation allowance, on all our deferred tax asset. In 2020, foreign (Greece and United Kingdom) valuation allowances were released, aggregating $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1892" name="us-gaap:ValuationAllowancesAndReservesDeductions" contextRef="From2020-01-01to2020-12-31" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">200,000</ix:nonFraction>, but has since reinstated all of the valuation allowances in the current year amounting to $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1897" name="us-gaap:ValuationAllowancesAndReservesDeductions" contextRef="From2022-01-01to2022-12-31" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">2,372,000</ix:nonFraction> during 2022. Management considered all available evidence to when evaluating the realizability of foreign deferred tax assets by jurisdiction and concluded primarily based upon a strong earnings history that these deferred tax assets were more-likely-than-not realizable.</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">The Company applied the &#8220;more-likely-than-not&#8221; recognition threshold to all tax positions taken or expected to be taken in a tax return, which resulted in no unrecognized tax benefits as of December 31, 2022 and December 31, 2021, respectively. We recognize interest accrued related to unrecognized tax benefits and penalties as income tax expense.</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">The Company files income tax returns in Illinois, United States, and in foreign jurisdictions including Greece, and United Kingdom. As of December 31, 2022, all domestic tax years are open to tax authority examination due the availability of net operating loss deductions, 2010 through 2022. In Greece, the statute of limitations is open for five years, 2017 through 2022. In United Kingdom, the statute of limitations is open for four years, 2018 through 2022. Currently, there are no ongoing tax authority income tax examinations.</p></ix:continuation><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><table style="border-spacing:0;font:10pt times new roman;width:100%" cellpadding="0"><tbody><tr style="height:15px"><td class="hpbhr"></td></tr><tr style="height:15px"><td style="BORDER-BOTTOM: black 1px solid; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:center;"><strong>December 31, 2022</strong></p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:center;">&#160;</p><ix:nonNumeric id="fid_956" name="us-gaap:RelatedPartyTransactionsDisclosureTextBlock" contextRef="From2022-01-01to2022-12-31" escape="true" continuedAt="cont_408266"><p style="font-size:10pt;font-family:times new roman;margin:0px"><strong>NOTE 9 &#8211; RELATED PARTY TRANSACTIONS </strong></p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;"><em>Doc Pharma S.A.</em></p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">As of December 31, 2022, the Company has a prepaid balance of $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1727" name="us-gaap:PrepaidInterest" contextRef="AsOf2022-12-31_cosm_DocPharmaSaMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">3,320,345</ix:nonFraction> and an accounts payable balance of $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1724" name="us-gaap:AccountsPayableOtherCurrent" contextRef="AsOf2022-12-31_cosm_DocPharmaSaMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">201,991</ix:nonFraction> to Doc Pharma S.A. related to purchases of inventory. Additionally, the Company has a receivable balance of $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1725" name="us-gaap:AccountsReceivableNetCurrent" contextRef="AsOf2022-12-31_cosm_DocPharmaSaMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">2,070,570</ix:nonFraction>. As of December 31, 2021, the Company has a prepaid balance of $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1728" name="us-gaap:PrepaidInterest" contextRef="AsOf2021-12-31_cosm_DocPharmaSaMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">3,263,241</ix:nonFraction> to Doc Pharma S.A. related to purchases of inventory. Additionally, the Company had a receivable balance of $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1726" name="us-gaap:AccountsReceivableNetCurrent" contextRef="AsOf2021-12-31_cosm_DocPharmaSaMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">2,901,300</ix:nonFraction> and an accounts payable balance of $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1737" name="us-gaap:AccountsPayableOtherCurrent" contextRef="AsOf2021-12-31_cosm_DocPharmaSaMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">565,756</ix:nonFraction>.</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">During the years ended December 31, 2022 and 2021, the Company purchased a total of $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1722" name="cosm:PaymentsToPurchaseProducts" contextRef="From2022-01-01to2022-12-31_cosm_DocPharmaSaMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">1,755,103</ix:nonFraction> and $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1723" name="cosm:PaymentsToPurchaseProducts" contextRef="From2021-01-01to2021-12-31_cosm_DocPharmaSaMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">3,084,805</ix:nonFraction> of products from Doc Pharma S.A., respectively. During the years ended December 31, 2022 and 2021 the Company had $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1731" name="us-gaap:Revenues" contextRef="From2022-01-01to2022-12-31_cosm_DocPharmaSaMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">1,058,780</ix:nonFraction> and $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1732" name="us-gaap:Revenues" contextRef="From2021-01-01to2021-12-31_cosm_DocPharmaSaMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">978,321</ix:nonFraction> revenue from Doc Pharma S.A., respectively.</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">On October 10, 2020, the Company entered into a contract manufacturer outsourcing (&#8220;CMO&#8221;) agreement with Doc Pharma whereby Doc Pharma is responsible for the development and manufacturing of pharmaceutical products and nutritional supplements according to the Company&#8217;s specifications based on strict pharmaceutical standards and good manufacturing practice (&#8220;GMP&#8221;) protocols as the National Organization for Medicines requires. The Company has the exclusive ownership rights for trading and distribution of its own branded nutritional supplements named &#8220;Sky Premium Life<sup style="vertical-align:super">&#174;</sup>&#8221;. The duration of the agreement is for <ix:nonNumeric id="fid_1733" name="cosm:AgreementTerm" contextRef="From2022-01-01to2022-12-31_cosm_DocPharmaSaMember" format="ixt-sec:duryear">5</ix:nonNumeric> years however either party may terminate the agreement at any time giving six-months advance notice. Doc Pharma is exclusively responsible for supplying the raw materials and packaging required to manufacture the final product. However, they are not responsible for potential delays that may arise, concerning their import. Doc Pharma is obliged to store the raw and packaging materials. The delivery of raw and packaging materials should be purchased at least 30 and 25 days, respectively, before the delivery date of the final product. The Manufacturer solely delivers the finished product to the Company. There is a minimum order quantity (&#8220;MoQ&#8221;) of <ix:nonNumeric id="fid_1734" name="cosm:PiecesPerProduct" contextRef="From2022-01-01to2022-12-31_cosm_DocPharmaSaMember">1,000 pieces</ix:nonNumeric> per product code. Both parties have agreed that the Company will deposit 60% of the total cost upon agreement and assignment and 40% of the total cost including VAT charge upon the delivery date. The prices are indicative and are subject to amendments if the cost of the raw material or the production cost change. </p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">For the years ended December 31, 2022 and 2021, the Company has purchased &#8364;1,653,911 ($<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1735" name="cosm:InventroyPurchase" contextRef="AsOf2022-12-31_cosm_DocPharmaSaMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">1,742,282</ix:nonFraction>) and &#8364;1,699,507 ($<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1738" name="cosm:InventroyPurchase" contextRef="AsOf2021-12-31_cosm_DocPharmaSaMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">2,010,517</ix:nonFraction>), respectively, in inventory related to this agreement.</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">On May 17, 2021, Doc Pharma and the Company entered into a Research and Development &#8220;R&amp;D&#8221; agreement whereby Doc Pharma will be responsible for the research, development, design, registration, copy rights and licenses of 250 nutritional supplements for the final products called Sky Premium Life<sup style="vertical-align:super">&#174;</sup>. These products will be sold in Greece and abroad. The total cost of this project will be &#8364;1,425,000 plus VAT and will be done over three phases as follows: <ix:nonNumeric id="fid_1739" name="us-gaap:ManagementFeeDescription" contextRef="From2022-05-01to2022-05-17_cosm_DocPharmaSaMember">Design &amp; Development (&#8364;725,000); Control and Product Manufacturing (&#8364;250,000) and Clinical Study and Research (&#8364;450,000). In the year ended December 31, 2021, SkyPharm bought 67 licenses at value of &#8364;261,300 ($289,860) from Doc Pharma which was the 18.33% of the total cost.</ix:nonNumeric> During the year ended December 31, 2022, <ix:nonNumeric id="fid_1736" name="us-gaap:ManagementFeeDescription" contextRef="From2022-01-01to2022-12-31_cosm_DocPharmaSaMember">SkyPharm bought another 14 licenses at a value of &#8364;293,200 ($313,665) from Doc Pharma which was the 20.57% of the total cost. SkyPharm has bought in total as of December 31, 2022, 81 licenses at value of &#8364;554,500 ($593,204) which is 38.91% of the total cost. The agreement will be terminated on December 31, 2025.</ix:nonNumeric>&#160;&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">The balance of prepaid expenses due to SkyPharm SA due Doc Pharma SA as of December 31, 2022, had increased to &#8364;7,103,706 ($<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1740" name="us-gaap:OtherExpenses" contextRef="From2022-01-01to2022-12-31" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">7,599,545</ix:nonFraction>), which was mainly attributable to the prepayments SkyPharm SA made in accordance with the CMO agreement and the extensive orders and sales of the SPL products the Company expects to achieve within 2023, mainly through its Amazon channels in UK, Singapore, Canada and other countries. However, as the benefit from a significant portion of the prepaid balance would not have been realized within a 12-month period, the Company opted to secure a portion of the outstanding prepaid balance through a loan agreement. On January 1, 2023, SkyPharm S.A. (the &#8220;Lender&#8221;) entered into a loan agreement with Doc Pharma S.A. (the &#8220;Borrower&#8221;) for &#8364;4,000,000 ($<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1742" name="us-gaap:PrepaidInterest" contextRef="AsOf2022-01-01_cosm_DocPharmaSaMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">4,279,200</ix:nonFraction>), all of which was financed through the outstanding prepaid balance. The duration of the loan will be for a 10-year period up to December 1, 2032 (&#8220;the Maturity date&#8221;). The loan will bear a fixed interest rate of <ix:nonFraction id="fid_1743" name="us-gaap:LineOfCreditFacilityInterestRateDuringPeriod" contextRef="From2022-01-01to2022-12-31_cosm_DocPharmaSaMember" unitRef="Pure" decimals="INF" scale="-2">5.5</ix:nonFraction>% payable on a monthly basis and will be repayable in 120 equal instalments of &#8364;33,333.33 ($35,660). The loan may be prepaid anytime during its in full or partially based on the Company&#8217;s product requirements and other factors, without Doc Pharma incurring any prepayment penalty. As of December 31, 2022, the loan had a current portion of &#8364;400,000 ($<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1756" name="cosm:LoanCurrentPortion" contextRef="AsOf2022-12-31_cosm_DocPharmaSaMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">427,920</ix:nonFraction>) and a non-current portion of &#8364;3,600,000 ($<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1757" name="cosm:LoanNonCurrentPortion" contextRef="AsOf2022-12-31_cosm_DocPharmaSaMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">3,851,280</ix:nonFraction>), which was reclassified to Loans receivable &#8211; related party as of December 31, 2022.&#160;</p></ix:nonNumeric><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;&#160;</p><table style="border-spacing:0;font:10pt times new roman;width:100%" cellpadding="0"><tbody><tr style="height:15px"><td class="hpbhr"></td></tr><tr style="height:15px"><td style="BORDER-BOTTOM: black 1px solid; TEXT-INDENT: 0px;text-align:center;">F-26</td></tr><tr style="height:15px"><td><p style='page-break-after: always'></p></td></tr><tr style="height:15px"><td></td></tr></tbody></table><p style="font-size:10pt;font-family:times new roman;margin:0px">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:center;"><strong>COSMOS HEALTH INC.</strong></p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:center;"><strong>Notes to the Consolidated Financial Statements</strong></p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:center;"><strong>December 31, 2022</strong></p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:center;">&#160;</p><ix:continuation id="cont_408266" continuedAt="cont_599231"><p style="font-size:10pt;font-family:times new roman;margin:0px">Doc Pharma S.A is considered a related party to the Company due to the fact that the CEO of Doc Pharma is the wife of Grigorios Siokas, the Company&#8217;s CEO and principal shareholder, who also served as a principal of Doc Pharma S.A. in the past.</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;"><em>Panagiotis Kozaris</em></p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">From time-to-time purchases back shares that Panagiotis Kozaris owns and records them as treasury shares. The Company pays Panagiotis Kozaris in advance for the shares owned and obtains the shares upon execution of a cumulative stock-purchase agreement (&#8220;SPA&#8221;). During 2021 the Company prepaid a total of $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1745" name="us-gaap:PrepaidInterest" contextRef="AsOf2022-12-31_cosm_PanagiotisKozarisMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">376,901</ix:nonFraction> to Panagiotis Kozaris and executed a cumulative SPA on December 29, 2021, upon which shares of treasury stock were obtained by the Company, reducing Panagiotis Kozaris&#8217; prepaid expense balance to $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1746" name="cosm:OtherPrepaidExpenses" contextRef="From2022-01-01to2022-12-31_cosm_PanagiotisKozarisMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">0</ix:nonFraction> as of December 31, 2021. During the year ended December 31, 2022 the Company paid Panagiotis Kozaris an additional sum of $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1744" name="cosm:SharesOwned" contextRef="From2022-01-01to2022-12-31_cosm_PanagiotisKozarisMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="Shares" decimals="0">143,056</ix:nonFraction> for shares owned, however, no SPA for these funds has been executed as of December 31, 2022. The amount of $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1747" name="cosm:OtherPrepaidExpenses" contextRef="From2022-05-01to2022-05-17_cosm_PanagiotisKozarisMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">143,056</ix:nonFraction> is included in Prepaid expenses and other current assets &#8211; related party, on the accompanying consolidated balance sheets as of December 31, 2022. The Company intends to execute a cumulative SPA for these amounts during Q2 2023.</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">Panagiotis Kozaris is considered a related party due to the fact that he is a former General operational manager and current employee of Cosmofarm SA.</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;"><em>Maria Kozari</em></p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">During 2021, the Company, through its subsidiary, Cosmofarm SA, commenced a partnership with a pharmacy called &#8220;Pharmacy &amp; More&#8221;, owned by Maria Kozari. The transactions with the respective pharmacy were in Cosmofarm&#8217;s normal course of business, however, a more flexible credit policy was allowed as the pharmacy was new and needed to be established in the market. During the years ended December 31, 2022 and 2021 the Company&#8217;s net sales to Pharmacy &amp; More amounted to $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1748" name="cosm:NetSales" contextRef="From2022-01-01to2022-12-31_cosm_MariaKozariMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">463,467</ix:nonFraction> and $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1749" name="cosm:NetSales" contextRef="From2021-01-01to2021-12-31_cosm_MariaKozariMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">358,524</ix:nonFraction> respectively. As of December 31, 2022 and 2021 the Company&#8217;s outstanding receivable balance due from the pharmacy amounted to $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1750" name="us-gaap:AccountsReceivableNetCurrent" contextRef="AsOf2022-12-31_cosm_MariaKozariMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">760,025</ix:nonFraction> (&#8364;710,436) and $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1751" name="us-gaap:AccountsReceivableNetCurrent" contextRef="AsOf2021-12-31_cosm_MariaKozariMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">366,270</ix:nonFraction> (&#8364;366,270), respectively, and are included in Accounts receivable - related party, on the accompanying consolidated balance sheets. The Company has recorded a cumulative bad debt allowance of $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1752" name="cosm:CumulativeBadDebtAllowance" contextRef="From2022-01-01to2022-12-31_cosm_MariaKozariMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">59,957</ix:nonFraction> to the outstanding balance due from Pharmacy &amp; More as of December 31, 2022, with the reserve for the period ended December 31, 2022 amounting to $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1753" name="us-gaap:PrepaidInterest" contextRef="AsOf2022-12-31_cosm_MariaKozariMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">48,230</ix:nonFraction>.</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">The Company plans to acquire Pharmacy &amp; More within fiscal year 2023, however the acquisition is conditional upon waiver of certain local legal restrictions. Upon acquisition, the Company intends to offset the outstanding receivable balance with the corresponding purchase price.</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;&#160;</p><p style="font-size:10pt;font-family:times new roman;margin:0px">Maria Kozari is considered a related party to the Company due to the fact that she is the daughter of Panagiotis Kozaris, a former Operational General Manager and current employee of Cosmofarm SA.</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">On December 20, 2018, the &#8364;1,500,000 ($<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1699" name="us-gaap:BorrowingsUnderGuaranteedInvestmentAgreements" contextRef="AsOf2018-12-20_cosm_GrigoriosSiokasMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">1,718,400</ix:nonFraction>) note payable, originally borrowed pursuant to a Loan Agreement with a third-party lender, dated March 16, 2018, was transferred to Grigorios Siokas. The note bears an interest rate of <ix:nonFraction id="fid_1715" name="us-gaap:LineOfCreditFacilityInterestRateDuringPeriod" contextRef="From2018-01-01to2018-12-20_cosm_GrigoriosSiokasMember" unitRef="Pure" decimals="INF" scale="-2">4.7</ix:nonFraction>% per annum,&#160;matured on <ix:nonNumeric id="fid_1717" name="us-gaap:DebtInstrumentMaturityDate" contextRef="From2018-01-01to2018-12-20_cosm_GrigoriosSiokasMember" format="ixt:datemonthdayyearen">March 18, 2019</ix:nonNumeric> pursuant to the original agreement and was extended until December 31, 2021. The note is not in default and the maturity date has been extended again until December 31, 2023. As of December 31, 2021 the Company had an outstanding balance of &#8364;400,000 ($<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1701" name="us-gaap:NotesPayable" contextRef="AsOf2022-12-31_cosm_GrigoriosSiokasMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">452,720</ix:nonFraction>) and accrued interest of &#8364;177,313 ($200,683).&#160;The Company repaid the outstanding principal balance in full during the year ended December 31, 2022. As of December 31, 2022 the Company had accrued interest of &#8364;192,891 ($<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1754" name="us-gaap:AccruedLiabilitiesCurrent" contextRef="AsOf2022-12-31_cosm_GrigoriosSiokasMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">206,355</ix:nonFraction>) outstanding related to this loan.</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;Grigorios Siokas is the Company&#8217;s CEO and principal shareholder.</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;"><em>Dimitrios Goulielmos</em></p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">On November 21, 2014, the Company entered into an agreement with Dimitrios Goulielmos, as amended on November 4, 2016. Pursuant to the amendment, this loan has no maturity date and is non-interest bearing. As of December 31, 2021, the Company had a principal balance of &#8364;10,200 ($<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1702" name="us-gaap:NotesPayable" contextRef="AsOf2021-12-31_cosm_DimitriosGoulielmosMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">11,544</ix:nonFraction>). A principal balance of &#8364;10,200 ($<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1703" name="us-gaap:NotesPayable" contextRef="AsOf2022-12-31_cosm_DimitriosGoulielmosMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">10,912</ix:nonFraction>) remained as of December 31, 2022.</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">Dimitrios Goulielmos is a former director and CEO of the Company.</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">The above balances are adjusted for the foreign currency rate as of the balance sheet date. For the years ended December 31, 2022, and 2021, the Company recorded a foreign currency translation loss of $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1707" name="us-gaap:ForeignCurrencyTransactionGainBeforeTax" FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;"><em>Grigorios Siokas</em></p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">From time to time, Grigorios Siokas loans the Company funds in the form of non-interest bearing, no-term loans. </p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">On May 10, 2021, the Company entered into a Debt Exchange agreement (&#8220;May Debt Exchange&#8221;) related to a lawsuit from on or about July 25, 2019, whereby Mark Rubenstein, individually and as a shareholder of the Company, brought the action styled <em>Rubenstein v. Siokas, et al.</em>, Case No. 1:19-cv-06976-KPF (S.D.N.Y.) against Grigorios Siokas for recovery of alleged profits earned under Section 16(b) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934. Although recovery was sought only from Mr. Siokas, the Company was also named as a nominal defendant. Both the Company and Mr. Siokas vigorously defended the lawsuit. On or about September, 18, 2020, in an effort to avoid the uncertainty of litigation and further legal expense, Mr. Siokas agreed to settle the lawsuit by agreeing to reimburse the Company a total of $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1719" name="us-gaap:LitigationSettlementInterest" contextRef="From2021-05-01to2021-05-10_cosm_GrigoriosSiokasMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">600,000</ix:nonFraction>, payable as a combination of: (1) Mr. Siokas reimbursing the Company for Plaintiff&#8217;s attorneys&#8217; fees, in an amount subsequently determined by the Court to be $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1720" name="cosm:PlantiffAttorneyFees" contextRef="From2021-05-01to2021-05-10_cosm_GrigoriosSiokasMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">120,000</ix:nonFraction> plus $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1721" name="cosm:LitigationSettlementAmount" contextRef="From2021-05-01to2021-05-10_cosm_GrigoriosSiokasMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">4,137</ix:nonFraction> of litigation costs to be paid in cash, and (2) Mr. Siokas relieving the Company of certain debt owed to him. Mr. Siokas and the Company strongly opposed Plaintiff&#8217;s motion for attorneys&#8217; fees. Pursuant to the terms of the May Debt Exchange the Company forgave $600,000 of the existing loan payable and recorded the forgiveness to additional paid in capital.&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">During the year ended December 31, 2021, the Company entered into various agreements (as defined in Note 7) with <ix:nonNumeric id="fid_1712" name="cosm:ConvertibleShareDescription" contextRef="From2022-01-01to2022-12-31_cosm_GrigoriosSiokasThreeMember">Mr. Siokas whereby the Company exchanged an aggregate total of $6,000,000 of debt into 40,000 shares of Common Stock at above market prices.</ix:nonNumeric> </p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">During the year ended December 31, 2021, the Company borrowed additional proceeds of &#8364;1,803,000 ($<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1700" name="us-gaap:BorrowingsUnderGuaranteedInvestmentAgreements" contextRef="AsOf2022-12-31_cosm_GrigoriosSiokasThreeMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">2,040,635</ix:nonFraction>), &#8364;230,000 ($<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1705" name="cosm:AdditionalProceedFromDebt" contextRef="From2022-01-01to2022-12-31_cosm_GrigoriosSiokasThreeMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">275,306</ix:nonFraction>) and $4,061,215 and repaid &#8364;118,000 ($<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1706" name="us-gaap:RepaymentsOfConstructionLoansPayable" contextRef="From2022-01-01to2022-12-31_cosm_GrigoriosSiokasThreeMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">133,552</ix:nonFraction>) of these loans. During the year ending December 31, 2021, the Company converted $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1713" name="us-gaap:DebtConversionConvertedInstrumentAmount1" contextRef="From2022-01-01to2022-12-31_cosm_GrigoriosSiokasThreeMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">2,250,000</ix:nonFraction> of the July 20 Note at a conversion price of $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1711" name="us-gaap:DebtInstrumentConvertibleConversionPrice1" contextRef="AsOf2022-12-31_cosm_GrigoriosSiokasThreeMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USDPShares" decimals="INF">150.00</ix:nonFraction> and issued <ix:nonFraction id="fid_1714" name="us-gaap:CommonStockSharesIssued" contextRef="AsOf2022-12-31_cosm_GrigoriosSiokasThreeMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="Shares" decimals="0">15,000</ix:nonFraction> shares of common stock. As of December 31, 2021, the Company had an outstanding balance under these notes and loans of $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1704" name="us-gaap:NotesPayable" contextRef="AsOf2022-12-31_cosm_GrigoriosSiokasThreeMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">1,293,472</ix:nonFraction>. </p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">During the year ended December 31, 2022, the Company borrowed additional proceeds of &#8364;656,750 ($<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1741" name="us-gaap:BorrowingsUnderGuaranteedInvestmentAgreements" contextRef="AsOf2022-01-01_cosm_GrigoriosSiokasMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">702,591</ix:nonFraction>), and $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1755" name="us-gaap:BorrowingsUnderGuaranteedInvestmentAgreements" contextRef="AsOf2021-12-31_cosm_GrigoriosSiokasMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">2,933,165</ix:nonFraction> and repaid &#8364;1,688,800 ($<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1729" name="cosm:RepaidAmount" contextRef="From2022-01-01to2022-12-31" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">1,806,678</ix:nonFraction>) and $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1730" name="cosm:RepaidAmount" contextRef="From2021-01-01to2021-12-31" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">3,045,000</ix:nonFraction> of these loans.</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">The above balances are adjusted for the foreign currency rate as of the balance sheet date. The outstanding balance of the facility as of December 31, 2022 and 2021, was $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1783" name="cosm:OutstandingDebtBalance" contextRef="AsOf2022-12-31_cosm_PancretaOfGreeceMember_us-gaap_LineOfCreditMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">1,232,128</ix:nonFraction> and $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1784" name="cosm:OutstandingDebtBalance" contextRef="AsOf2021-12-31_cosm_PancretaOfGreeceMember_us-gaap_LineOfCreditMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">489,985</ix:nonFraction>, respectively. </p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">The Company entered into a line of credit with EGF on June 6, 2022. The line of credit is renewed annually with a current interest rate of 4.49%. The maximum borrowing allowed as of December 31, 2022 was $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1776" name="us-gaap:BorrowingsUnderGuaranteedInvestmentAgreements" contextRef="AsOf2022-12-31_cosm_EFGMember_us-gaap_LineOfCreditMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">427,920</ix:nonFraction>. The outstanding balance of the facility as of December 31, 2022 was $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1785" name="us-gaap:LineOfCreditFacilityAverageOutstandingAmount" contextRef="From2022-01-01to2022-12-31_cosm_EFGMember_us-gaap_LineOfCreditMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">431,512</ix:nonFraction>.</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">Under the agreements, the Company is required to maintain certain financial ratios and covenants. These lines of credit were assumed in the Company&#8217;s acquisition of Cosmofarm. As of December 31, 2022 and 2021, the Company was in compliance with these ratios and covenants. </p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">The above lines of credit are guaranteed and backed by customer receivable checks and they are not considered to be a direct debt obligation for the Company. They are a type of factoring, where the postponed customer checks are assigned by the Company to the bank, in order to be financed at a pre-agreed rate. </p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">Interest expense for the years ended December 31, 2022 and 2021, was $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1787" name="us-gaap:InterestExpense" contextRef="From2022-01-01to2022-12-31_cosm_COVID19GovernmentFundingMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">294,156</ix:nonFraction> and $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1788" name="us-gaap:InterestExpense" contextRef="From2021-01-01to2021-12-31_cosm_COVID19GovernmentFundingMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">283,415</ix:nonFraction>, respectively.</p></ix:nonNumeric><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><ix:nonNumeric id="fid_958" name="cosm:ConvertibleDebtDisclosureTextBlock" contextRef="From2022-01-01to2022-12-31" escape="true" continuedAt="cont_17455c"><p MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">A summary of the Company&#8217;s convertible debt during the FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:center;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:center;"><strong>COSMOS HEALTH INC.</strong></p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:center;"><strong>Notes to the Consolidated Financial Statements</strong></p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:center;"><strong>December 31, 2022</strong></p><p style="font-size:10pt;font-family:times new roman;margin:0px">&#160;</p><ix:continuation id="cont_17455c" continuedAt="cont_ef82ed"><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;"><em>December 21, 2020 Securities Purchase Agreement</em></p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">On December 21, 2020 the Company entered into a convertible promissory note with Platinum Point Capital, LLC (the &#8220;Holder&#8221;, &#8220;Lender&#8221; or &#8220;Platinum&#8221;) pursuant to a Securities Purchase Agreement (the &#8220;SPA&#8221;).</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">The Company issued the $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1869" name="us-gaap:NotesIssued1" contextRef="From2020-12-01to2020-12-21_cosm_ConvertiblePromissoryNoteMember_cosm_SecuritiesPurchaseAgreementMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">540,000</ix:nonFraction>&#160;Note in exchange for $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1873" name="cosm:NoteIssuedUponExchangeForCash" contextRef="From2020-12-01to2020-12-21_cosm_ConvertiblePromissoryNoteMember_cosm_SecuritiesPurchaseAgreementMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">500,000</ix:nonFraction>&#160;in cash and included a $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1860" name="cosm:DebtOriginalIssueDiscount" contextRef="AsOf2020-12-21_cosm_ConvertiblePromissoryNoteMember_cosm_SecuritiesPurchaseAgreementMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">40,000</ix:nonFraction>&#160;Original Issue Discount (&#8220;OID&#8221;) and paid $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1863" name="cosm:FinancingCost" contextRef="From2020-12-01to2020-12-21_cosm_ConvertiblePromissoryNoteMember_cosm_SecuritiesPurchaseAgreementMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">3,000</ix:nonFraction>&#160;in financing costs. The principal amount together with interest at the rate of eight percent (<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1864" name="us-gaap:DebtConversionOriginalDebtInterestRateOfDebt" contextRef="From2020-12-01to2020-12-21_cosm_ConvertiblePromissoryNoteMember_cosm_SecuritiesPurchaseAgreementMember" unitRef="Pure" decimals="INF" scale="-2">8.0</ix:nonFraction>%) per annum, compounded annually (the &#8220;Interest Rate&#8221;), will be paid to the Lenders on or before the Maturity Date (December 31, 2021 or as defined below). Accrued interest shall be calculated on the basis of a 360-day year for the actual number of days elapsed. In the event that on or before the Maturity Date, the Note either (i) had not been converted or have not been otherwise satisfied in full or (ii) an Event of Default (as defined in the SPA) occurs, then the applicable rate of interest on the outstanding amount of the&#160;Note since inception shall be the Interest Rate plus eighteen percent (<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1866" name="us-gaap:DebtInstrumentInterestRateStatedPercentage" contextRef="AsOf2020-12-21_cosm_ConvertiblePromissoryNoteMember_cosm_SecuritiesPurchaseAgreementMember" unitRef="Pure" decimals="INF" scale="-2">18.0</ix:nonFraction>%), the Default Interest. Unless previously converted, the principal and accrued interest on the Note is due and payable in cash (USD) upon the earlier of (i) December 31, 2021, (ii) a Change of Control (as defined in the SPA) or (iii), an Event of Default (collectively, the &#8220;Maturity Date&#8221;).</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">During the year ended December 31, 2021, the Company converted an aggregate total of $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1838" name="us-gaap:ConvertibleNotesPayable" contextRef="AsOf2022-12-31_cosm_InstitutionalInvestorsMember_cosm_SeniorConvertibleNoteOneMember_cosm_SecuritiesPurchaseAgreementMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">525,000</ix:nonFraction>&#160;in principal and $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1885" name="us-gaap:InterestPayableCurrent" contextRef="AsOf2021-12-31_cosm_CommonStocksShareMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">25,144</ix:nonFraction>&#160;in accrued interest and fees into&#160;<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1886" name="cosm:FeesIntoCommonStockShares" contextRef="AsOf2021-12-31_cosm_CommonStocksShareMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="Shares" decimals="0">8,535</ix:nonFraction> shares of the Company&#8217;s common stock at an average price per share of $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1884" name="us-gaap:SharePrice" contextRef="AsOf2021-12-31_cosm_CommonStocksShareMember" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USDPShares" decimals="INF">64.50</ix:nonFraction>.
